- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

aiTitle: Fix multiplier increment for simultaneous target eliminations

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Found root cause of duplicate position increment issue in ExplodeState</title>
  <subtitle>BuildUnifiedExplodeData deduplicates positions using Set, causing same position to only increment once</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>ExplodeState.BuildUnifiedExplodeData uses Set to deduplicate elimination positions from CurExplodeDataVec</fact>
    <fact>Deduplicated positions are grouped by symbol and stored in explodeDataVec</fact>
    <fact>Multiplier base increment loop dispatches ADD_MULTIPLIER_WITH_ANIM once per position in explodeDataVec</fact>
    <fact>When two targets eliminate same position, Set deduplication causes position to appear only once in final data</fact>
    <fact>Current logic increments each eliminated position by exactly 1, regardless of how many times it was targeted</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The primary session examined ExplodeState.ts and identified the root cause of the reported bug. The BuildUnifiedExplodeData method (lines 137-169) intentionally deduplicates positions using a Set data structure to prevent duplicate visual effects when the same position is eliminated by multiple sources (targets or awards). However, this deduplication also affects the multiplier base increment logic. At lines 102-113, the code dispatches ADD_MULTIPLIER_WITH_ANIM for each unique position in explodeDataVec, meaning each position increments by exactly 1. When two targets eliminate the same position simultaneously, that position should increment by 2, but the current Set-based deduplication causes it to only increment by 1. The fix will need to either track elimination counts before deduplication or handle multiplier base increments separately from the unified explosion visual data.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>assets/Script/GameState/ExplodeState.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

aiTitle: Fix multiplier increment for simultaneous target eliminations

content.thinking:
The user is examining MultiplierBaseManager.ts to understand the multiplier base increment system. Looking at the code:

1. ADD_MULTIPLIER and ADD_MULTIPLIER_WITH_ANIM events exist
2. These methods add 1 to the multiplier index for a position
3. The issue is that when the same position is eliminated multiple times (by different targets), it should increment by 2 (or more) but currently only increments by 1

The key finding here is that ADD_MULTIPLIER_WITH_ANIM takes an index and increments it by exactly 1. There's no parameter for adding by a custom amount. This confirms the issue - the system is designed to add 1 per position, but doesn't support adding multiple increments when the same position is eliminated multiple times.

This is a discovery observation showing the current implementation and confirming the limitation.

content.thinking:
The user is asking me to observe a primary session where someone is investigating an issue with multiplier base increments. Let me analyze what has been observed:

1. The user's request (in Chinese) is about:
   - When symbols are eliminated, the multiplier base at each eliminated position should increase by 1
   - But if the same position is eliminated twice simultaneously (by 2 targets), it should increase by 2
   - Currently with 2 targets, there's a chance they eliminate the same position, but it's only incrementing by 1

2. The session has read several files to understand the system:
   - CountMultState.ts - processes elimination data from TargetDataVec and AwardDataVec
   - ExplodeState.ts - has BuildUnifiedExplodeData that uses a Set to deduplicate positions
   - MultiplierBaseManager.ts - provides ADD_MULTIPLIER_WITH_ANIM that only increments by 1
   - Proto definition files showing TargetData and AwardData structures
   - WantedPosterState.ts and MatchingPatchUpState.ts showing how the game flow works

3. The grep searches were looking at:
   - Proto definitions for TargetData, AwardData, EliminatePos
   - Server data structures like BasePlateIndex
   - How ADD_MULTIPLIER_WITH_ANIM and SET_MULTIPLIER events are used

The key discovery is that:
- BuildUnifiedExplodeData in ExplodeState uses a Set to deduplicate positions (line 147-169)
- This is intentional to prevent duplicate visual effects
- But it also causes positions to only appear once in the final explodeDataVec
- The increment loop (lines 102-113 in ExplodeState) then only increments each unique position once
- There's no tracking of how many times a position was eliminated before deduplication

This is investigation/discovery work - no code has been modified yet. 

The session is now working through the problem systematically, examining the root cause before determining what needs to change.
